heading: Uniform allowances
label: 5901 Uniform allowances
notes: Historical and Revision Notes1966ActDerivationU.S. CodeRevised Statutes andStatutes at Large(a), (b).5 U.S.C. 2131, .Sept. 1, 1954, ch. 1208, § 40268 Stat. 1114, .May 13, 1955, ch. 4069 Stat. 49(c)(less applicability to the Civil Service Retirement Act, as amended).5 U.S.C. 2132(less applicability to the Civil Service Retirement Act, as amended), .Sept. 1, 1954, ch. 1208, § 40368 Stat. 1115(d).5 U.S.C. 2133, .Sept. 1, 1954, ch. 1208, § 40468 Stat. 1115In subsection (a), the word “concerned” is substituted for “to which any such appropriation is made”.In subsection (b), the words “in his discretion” are omitted as unnecessary in view of the permissive nature of the authority.In subsections (b) and (d), the word “rules” is omitted as covered by the word “regulations”.Standard changes are made to conform with the definitions applicable and the style of this title as outlined in the preface to the report.

In the last sentence of subsection (a), the words “When” and “pays” are substituted for “In those instances where” and “makes reimbursement”, respectively.5 U.S.C. 59015 U.S.C. 5903

Amendments1992—Subsec. (a)(1), (2). substituted “5902)” for “5902).”Pub. L. 102–3781990—Subsec. (a). , substituted “such sums as may be necessary to carry out this subchapter.” for “an amount not to exceed $125 multiplied by the number of employees of the agency who are required by regulation or statute to wear a prescribed uniform in the performance of official duties and who are not being furnished with the uniform.”Pub. L. 101–509, § 529 [title II, § 202(a)(1)]Subsec. (a)(1), (2). , substituted “$400 a year (or such higher maximum amount as the Office of Personnel Management may establish under section 5902).” for “$125 a year”.Pub. L. 101–509, § 529 [title II, § 202(a)(2)]

Effective Date of 1990 AmendmentAmendment by effective on such date as the President shall determine, but not earlier than 90 days, and not later than 180 days, after , see section 529 [title III, § 305] of , set out as a note under .Pub. children.text: furnish to each of these employees a uniform at a cost not to exceed $400 a year (or such higher maximum amount as the Office of Personnel Management may establish under section 5902); or
pay to each of these employees an allowance for a uniform not to exceed $400 a year (or such higher maximum amount as the Office of Personnel Management may establish under section 5902).

children.label: b
children.text: When the furnishing of a uniform or the payment of a uniform allowance is authorized under another statute or regulation existing on , the head of the agency concerned may continue the furnishing of the uniform or the payment of the uniform allowance under that statute or regulation, but in that event a uniform may not be furnished or allowance paid under this section.September 1, 1954

children.label: c
children.text: An allowance paid under this section is not wages within the meaning of or chapters 21 and 24 of title 26.section 409 of title 42
